result:
If I could change the world in one way, I would dissolve the illusion of separation—between people, nations, and nature—by awakening a deeper, shared understanding of our interdependence.  

This isn’t about erasing diversity or individuality, but about shifting the underlying story from “us versus them” and “humanity versus nature” to one of interconnected thriving.  

If that awareness truly took root, many of our most persistent problems—from conflict and inequality to environmental neglect—would begin to untangle, not through force or imposed rules, but through a fundamental shift in how we see ourselves and our place in the whole.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
